method.name: llm_reasoned
method.description: Compute statistical pairwise evidence, then ask an LLM to reason over stop-profile, representative route-image, and fleet-metadata evidence to produce final pairwise total_correlation values.

route_similarity.description: For each truck, choose the representative image from each route cluster, compare representative route-ink vectors by cosine similarity, and average pairwise similarities weighted by cluster shares.

method.global_rationale: Similarities were evaluated by weighting stop-profile correlation (0.45 weight) as the primary differentiator of zone-pass behavior, with duty-cycle and purpose matches providing categorical alignment. kms similarity indicates operational scale parity. Route similarity from representative images is consistently weak across all pairs, suggesting fleet trucks serve overlapping zones through distinct route clusters. Negative stop-profile correlations (pairs 1688, 1785, 1311, 1423) indicate antagonistic zone-pass patterns—likely opposite directions, times, or service models within the fleet. Purpose mismatches and duty-cycle failures create hard barriers to scenario transferability despite moderate statistical scores. Age similarity is uniformly weak, suggesting fleet age diversity does not drive behavioral clustering.
risks: Route similarity metric uniformly weak (max 0.19); image-based PCA may not capture behavioral route distinctions adequately.
Negative stop-profile correlations suggest zone antagonism; scenario generation from such pairs may produce contradictory stop patterns.
Purpose and duty-cycle mismatches override continuous metrics; categorical alignment is essential but not captured in statistical aggregation.
All 31-day observation windows are identical; temporal variation in behavior patterns cannot be assessed.
kms similarity does not account for stop density, dwell time, or service intensity—two trucks with similar daily km may have very different operational rhythms.

pairwise_argument_correlations.llm_rationale: Strong stop-profile and duty-cycle match (both 1.0 purpose and duty), good kms similarity (0.51), but route similarity is very weak (0.048). Statistical score of 0.594 is appropriate.

llm_feature_interpretation.stop_profile_correlation: 0.63 indicates moderate-to-good alignment in zone-pass patterns across weekday/weekend.
llm_feature_interpretation.route_similarity: 0.048 is very weak; trucks operate in different route clusters despite shared zones.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Perfect purpose and duty-cycle match (1.0 each) and age similarity (0.28) support behavioral similarity despite route divergence.

pairwise_argument_correlations.llm_rationale: Strong stop-profile (0.57), perfect purpose/duty match, good kms similarity (0.66). Route similarity weak (0.046). Statistical score of 0.581 is appropriate.

llm_feature_interpretation.route_similarity: 0.046 suggests distinct route clusters despite behavioral overlap.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Purpose and duty-cycle both 1.0; kms similarity 0.66 indicates comparable operational scale.

pairwise_argument_correlations.llm_rationale: Moderate stop-profile (0.49), perfect purpose/duty match, excellent kms similarity (0.68), but weak route similarity (0.045). Statistical 0.547 is reasonable.

llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Perfect purpose and duty-cycle; kms 0.68 suggests similar operational distance.

pairwise_argument_correlations.llm_rationale: Very strong stop-profile (0.86) and duty-cycle match (1.0) partially offset by zero purpose match and weak kms (0.37). Adjust down slightly from 0.562 due to purpose mismatch limiting scenario compatibility.

llm_feature_interpretation.stop_profile_correlation: 0.86 is high; trucks share zone-pass patterns.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Purpose mismatch (0.0) is significant; despite zone similarity, different truck types limit behavioral transferability.

pairwise_argument_correlations.llm_rationale: Strong stop-profile (0.71), excellent kms (0.80), perfect duty-cycle, but zero purpose match and very weak route similarity (0.029). Adjust down slightly from 0.530 due to purpose mismatch.

llm_feature_interpretation.route_similarity: 0.029 is very weak; distinct route clusters.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Purpose mismatch (0.0) limits scenario applicability despite strong operational metrics.

pairwise_argument_correlations.llm_rationale: Strong stop-profile (0.71), good age similarity (0.39), good kms (0.57), but zero purpose match and weak route similarity (0.036). Statistical 0.523 is appropriate.

llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Purpose mismatch (0.0); age similarity 0.39 is the highest in this group but insufficient to overcome purpose divergence.

pairwise_argument_correlations.llm_rationale: Strong stop-profile (0.68), excellent kms (0.82), perfect duty-cycle, but zero purpose match and very weak route similarity (0.025). Purpose mismatch limits scenario fit.

llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Purpose mismatch (0.0); kms similarity 0.82 is strong but offset by purpose divergence.

pairwise_argument_correlations.llm_rationale: Good stop-profile (0.65), exceptional kms similarity (0.97), perfect duty-cycle, but zero purpose match and weak route similarity (0.034). Kms strength partially offset by purpose mismatch.

llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: kms similarity 0.97 is exceptional, but purpose mismatch (0.0) limits transferability.

pairwise_argument_correlations.llm_rationale: Strong stop-profile (0.74), weak kms (0.33), weak route similarity (0.082), zero purpose match. Statistical 0.509 is acceptable; adjust slightly up due to stronger stop-profile.

llm_feature_interpretation.route_similarity: 0.082 is weak but highest in lower-correlation group.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Purpose mismatch and low kms similarity indicate distinct operational profiles.

pairwise_argument_correlations.llm_rationale: Strong stop-profile (0.74), weak kms (0.39), weak route similarity (0.034), zero purpose match. Statistical 0.505 is appropriate.

llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Purpose mismatch and weak kms limit scenario applicability.

pairwise_argument_correlations.llm_rationale: Moderate stop-profile (0.63), excellent kms (0.82), but zero purpose match and weak route similarity (0.035). Statistical 0.490 reflects appropriate weighting.

llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Purpose mismatch despite strong kms similarity; different truck types.

pairwise_argument_correlations.llm_rationale: Moderate stop-profile (0.64), weak kms (0.24), weak route similarity (0.036), zero purpose match. Lower kms and weak route profile justify lower total score.

llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Weak kms (0.24) and purpose mismatch indicate low operational similarity.

pairwise_argument_correlations.llm_rationale: Weak stop-profile (0.45), weak kms (0.31), weak route similarity (0.083), zero purpose match. Statistical 0.383 appropriately reflects low overall similarity.

llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Weak across all operational dimensions; different use cases.

pairwise_argument_correlations.llm_rationale: Negative stop-profile correlation (-0.12) indicates opposing zone-pass patterns. Perfect purpose/duty match cannot overcome behavioral divergence. Adjust up slightly from 0.252 due to purpose match providing some scenario overlap, but remain conservative.

llm_feature_interpretation.stop_profile_correlation: -0.12 indicates trucks operate in opposite zone profiles; likely serving different directions or service times.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Purpose and duty-cycle match (1.0 each) suggest same truck type, but zone antagonism indicates different operational patterns within same category.

pairwise_argument_correlations.llm_rationale: Negative stop-profile (-0.17) indicates opposing zone patterns. Perfect purpose/duty and moderate kms (0.52) cannot offset behavioral antagonism. Adjust down from 0.234.

llm_feature_interpretation.stop_profile_correlation: -0.17 indicates opposing zone-pass profiles; trucks serve different areas or times.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Same purpose/duty but contradictory zone behavior suggests different route assignments despite similar truck type.

pairwise_argument_correlations.llm_rationale: Zero stop-profile (no zone overlap), weak route similarity (0.073), zero purpose match, weak kms (0.54). Statistical 0.193 is appropriate; no basis for scenario transfer.

llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Zero purpose match and no zone overlap indicate completely separate operational profiles.

pairwise_argument_correlations.llm_rationale: Negative stop-profile (-0.20), excellent kms (0.80), weak route similarity (0.059), zero purpose match. Antagonistic zone patterns despite high kms similarity. Adjust up slightly from 0.126 due to kms strength.

llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: kms similarity 0.80 suggests same operational scale, but zone antagonism indicates fundamentally different routes.

pairwise_argument_correlations.llm_rationale: Negative stop-profile (-0.20), good kms (0.78), weak route similarity (0.061), zero purpose match. Zone antagonism dominant despite kms strength.

llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: kms similarity 0.78 but zone antagonism indicates opposite route directions or service areas.

pairwise_argument_correlations.llm_rationale: Zero stop-profile, zero duty-cycle match (critical), weak route similarity (0.191), zero purpose match. Duty-cycle mismatch indicates fundamentally different operational patterns.

llm_feature_interpretation.route_similarity: 0.191 is weak to moderate but insufficient.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Zero duty-cycle match is a red flag; trucks operate under different schedules or intensity patterns.
